title: Lost Faith.
content:
To lose one's faith surpasses
    The loss of an estate,
    Because estates can be
    Replenished, -- faith cannot.

    Inherited with life,
    Belief but once can be;
    Annihilate a single clause,
    And Being's beggary.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Lost Faith.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
